evals.prompt: I need help figuring out which tech stack to pick for my startup. We're 2 engineers, bootstrapped, building a SaaS dashboard for small restaurants to manage their inventory. We're debating between Next.js + Supabase vs Django + Postgres + React. I've been going back and forth for 3 weeks and can't decide.
evals.expected_output: Protocol runs full OEIP: detects DECISION query, assembles relevant roles (tech architect, startup strategist, at minimum), asks targeted questions about team strengths, timeline, scale expectations, then delivers multi-role synthesis with a clear specific recommendation, trade-offs, and next steps.

assertions.text: Response does NOT bypass the protocol — full OEIP runs (interrogation + roles + synthesis present)
assertions.name: protocol_runs_not_bypassed

assertions.text: Domain snapshot is shown (contains 'Domain:' and 'Query type:' and 'User level:' labels)
assertions.name: domain_snapshot_shown

assertions.text: At least 3 distinct expert roles are assembled (role boxes or role labels visible)
assertions.name: assembles_minimum_3_roles

assertions.text: Interrogation questions appear before Phase 2 synthesis (questions asked before 'ROLE LOCKED' sections)
assertions.name: interrogation_before_synthesis

assertions.text: Team Lead makes a specific recommendation for ONE stack (not 'both are valid' or 'it depends')
assertions.name: specific_stack_recommended

assertions.text: Section ⑩ IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS is present with at least 3 concrete numbered actions
assertions.name: includes_next_steps

assertions.text: Edge case or failure map table is present (section ⑤)
assertions.name: includes_failure_map

evals.prompt: I'm a 2nd year nursing student and I want to eventually specialize. I don't know if I should go into ICU nursing, pediatrics, or maybe become a nurse practitioner eventually. I want good pay, I don't mind stress but I need work-life balance eventually when I have kids, and I want to feel like my work matters. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Protocol detects DECISION+STRATEGY query with beginner user. Assembles 4-5 roles. Uses plain-language framing. Addresses all 3 stated priorities. Delivers a specific career path recommendation with glossary, alternative paths, and next steps.

assertions.text: Response does NOT bypass the protocol — full OEIP runs
assertions.name: protocol_runs_not_bypassed

assertions.text: A Glossary section is present (beginner user → glossary required)
assertions.name: includes_glossary

assertions.text: All 3 user priorities explicitly addressed: pay/income, work-life balance, meaningful work
assertions.name: three_priorities_addressed

assertions.text: Team Lead makes a specific career path recommendation (not 'explore all three options')
assertions.name: concrete_recommendation

assertions.text: Critical Assumptions section present (section ⑨) with at least 1 flagged assumption
assertions.name: includes_critical_assumptions

assertions.text: Response uses beginner-friendly language (nursing jargon is explained or avoided)
assertions.name: beginner_language_used

evals.expected_output: Fitness check fires. Protocol bypassed. Direct answer given (4). No roles assembled. No interrogation questions asked. Response is short.

assertions.text: Protocol is BYPASSED — no interrogation questions asked
assertions.name: bypasses_protocol

assertions.text: No role assembly — does NOT contain 'ROLE LOCKED' or role box formatting
assertions.name: no_role_assembly
